title: Fraxswap - Open App
meta_description: fraxswap opens the official Frax swap app for token swaps, TWAMM orders, and liquidity routes across supported networks.
hero_h2: Token swaps through a TWAMM-native AMM
hero_lead: Fraxswap opens the official Frax interface for token swaps, TWAMM long-term orders, and liquidity actions on supported networks.
what_is: Fraxswap is Frax Finance's permissionless AMM with an embedded TWAMM for long-term orders; the official docs describe a Uniswap V2-style constant-product core extended for time-weighted execution.

how.d: Use the official Frax swap route, connect a Web3 wallet, and select the network you want to use.
Pick the input and output assets, then review the displayed route, price impact, and slippage settings.
Use a standard swap for immediate execution or review TWAMM order details when placing longer-term trades.
Approve the token if required, submit the transaction, and verify the result in your wallet or explorer.

points.p: The embedded TWAMM design lets long-term orders execute over time while interacting with the underlying AMM.
The core follows a Uniswap V2-style constant-product model, with technical details available in the Frax technical specifications.
The interface is suited for FRAX, FXS, and supported LP pairs across the deployments listed by Frax documentation.

faq.a: fraxswap is Frax Finance's AMM interface for token swaps and TWAMM-style long-term orders, built around a Uniswap V2-style constant-product core.
This page is a routing page; use the official app link and verify the domain before connecting a wallet or signing any transaction.
Yes. Frax documentation describes an embedded TWAMM for splitting long-term orders over time instead of executing the full amount at once.

faq.q: Which networks are listed for fraxswap V2?
faq.a: Frax documentation lists V2 deployments across networks including Ethereum, Fraxtal, Arbitrum, Avalanche, BSC, Fantom, Optimism, Polygon, Moonbeam, and Moonriver.

faq.q: Do I need ETH to use fraxswap on Ethereum?
faq.a: Yes. Ethereum transactions require ETH for gas, and other supported networks require their own native gas token.

footer_note: This page is not the official app; verify app.frax.finance before connecting. Educational only, not financial advice.
